Builds

Configuring this repository
Build specifications are authored in LabVIEW - this page does not change them. You create an executable, packed library, installer or other build in LabVIEW's Project Explorer (right-click Build Specifications › New, configure it, and Save) and commit the .lvproj. This page only tells the automated pipeline which of those build specifications to run headlessly, and on which operating system:
  • each build specification runs via LabVIEWCLI -OperationName ExecuteBuildSpec;
  • the built artifacts are attached to each run (and to the GitHub Release on a version tag);
  • Installer and .NET Interop builds are Windows-only.
Builds is not currently an enabled activity for this repository. These selections are saved, but nothing runs until Builds is turned on in Configure Pipeline › Pipeline.

Build specifications to run

Every build specification found in your projects is built by default. Untick a box to skip a specification on that operating system. A specification builds where its type is supported (Installer and .NET Interop are Windows-only).

Build specificationTypeWindowsLinux

Save and apply

With a valid dashboard token, save these settings by queuing the Reconfigure workflow. It updates only the Builds settings in .github/labview-ci.yml (every other setting is preserved) and opens a pull request to review. The command below is a backup if the browser token is missing or rejected.

View workflow
Saving needs a fine-grained token with Actions: Read and write for this repository.
Create a token
Command fallback

Use this backup path if the browser has no valid token, or if you prefer to dispatch the workflow from GitHub's CLI.
